findings.description: The callout asks whether [1, 2, 2, 3, 7, 7, 8, 10] (non-decreasing) is a valid max-heap, with a hint implying the answer relates to non-increasing sort; an array sorted in non-decreasing order has its smallest element at the root (H[1]=1) and largest at the end, which clearly violates the max-heap property (root must be maximum), so the answer is 'no' — but the hint 'consider what position 1 holds in a non-decreasing vs. non-increasing sort' is misleading because it suggests the answer might be nuanced, when in fact any non-decreasing sorted array with distinct elements trivially fails the max-heap property at the root.
